enrichment.short_description: A lightweight utility for installing and organizing marketing tracking pixels (Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, and similar ad-platform tags) on a website so teams can monitor clicks and conversions.
enrichment.seo_title: Clicks Pixels Manager: Marketing Pixel Tracking Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clicks Pixels Manager helps site owners install, organize, and manage ad-platform tracking pixels for click and conversion monitoring without editing site code.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Clicks Pixels Manager belongs to a category of marketing utilities commonly called pixel managers or tag managers. Tools in this category let website owners, marketers, and e-commerce operators add, organize, and fire tracking pixels — small snippets of code from advertising and analytics platforms such as the Meta (Facebook) Pixel, Google Ads conversion tracking, TikTok Pixel, and Google Analytics 4 — without manually editing a site's source code. The core job of a pixel manager is to reduce the technical overhead of conversion tracking: instead of asking a developer to hand-place multiple vendor scripts across a site, a marketer pastes in pixel IDs through a dashboard or lightweight interface, and the tool injects and fires the corresponding tracking events (page views, add-to-cart, purchase, lead form submission, and similar actions) at the right moments. This class of tool is typically aimed at small and mid-sized website owners, agencies managing pixels across multiple client sites, and e-commerce store operators who need clean, centralized conversion data to feed ad platforms and attribution reports. Independent, publicly documented details about a distinct product using the exact name "Clicks Pixels Manager" — such as its founding company, headquarters, release history, or a published pricing page — were not found in available public sources at the time of this review. Because of that, this summary is deliberately scoped to describing what tools in this product category generally do rather than asserting specific, unverifiable facts about a named vendor. Prospective users evaluating a tool under this name should confirm directly with the vendor or listing source what platforms it supports (Meta, Google, TikTok, Pinterest, Snapchat, etc.), whether it requires a content management system such as WordPress or Shopify, how pricing is structured, and what data-privacy or consent-management capabilities (e.g., GDPR/CCPA cookie consent gating) are included, since these vary widely between similarly named pixel and tag management utilities on the market. Buyers should also verify whether the tool supports server-side tracking (conversions APIs), which has become increasingly important as browsers restrict third-party cookies and ad platforms push advertisers toward first-party and server-side event tracking for reliable attribution.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Clicks Pixels Manager is listed as a tool in the marketing pixel/tag management category — software that helps website owners install and manage tracking pixels from advertising platforms without writing custom code.

Tools of this type typically center on a dashboard where a user enters pixel or tag IDs for platforms like Meta Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, and Google Analytics, and the tool handles firing the correct events on page views, form submissions, and purchases.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Typical capabilities in this product category include centralized pixel installation across multiple ad platforms, event-based firing rules (e.g., purchase, add-to-cart, lead submission), a no-code setup flow that avoids direct source-code edits, and a dashboard for reviewing which pixels are active on a site.

Specific feature availability for this particular listing has not been independently verified against a public product page; prospective users should confirm exact capabilities directly with the vendor before purchasing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: No publicly documented pricing page for a product under this exact name was found. Tools in this category commonly range from free, single-site plugins to paid plans priced per site or per tracked event for agencies managing pixels across multiple client properties.
Anyone evaluating this tool should request current pricing directly from the vendor rather than relying on category averages.

features.title: Centralized pixel installation
features.description: Add tracking pixel IDs for ad platforms in one place instead of editing site code for each vendor tag.

features.title: Multi-platform tag support
features.description: Common tools in this category support pixels from Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, and similar advertising networks.

features.title: Event-based firing
features.description: Pixels typically fire on specific site events such as page views, add-to-cart actions, and completed purchases.

features.title: No-code setup
features.description: Marketers can typically configure pixels through a dashboard without developer involvement.

pros: Reduces the need for manual code edits when adding ad-platform tracking pixels.
Centralizes pixel management, which is useful for agencies handling several client sites.
Lowers the technical barrier to conversion tracking for non-developer marketers.
cons: Public documentation, pricing, and company details for this specific listing are limited, making it hard to evaluate before contacting the vendor.
Feature depth (e.g., server-side/conversions API support, consent management) is unverified and should be confirmed directly.
Smaller, niche pixel tools can lag behind major tag-management platforms in update cadence and platform coverage.

faqs.question: What does Clicks Pixels Manager do?
faqs.answer: It falls into the category of pixel/tag management tools, which let website owners install and manage advertising tracking pixels (such as Meta or Google Ads pixels) without editing site code directly.

faqs.question: Which ad platforms does it support?
faqs.answer: Tools in this category commonly support major ad networks like Meta, Google Ads, and TikTok, but exact platform support for this specific listing should be confirmed with the vendor.

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Pricing has not been independently verified for this listing; contact the vendor for current plan and pricing details.

faqs.question: Do I need a developer to set it up?
faqs.answer: Pixel manager tools are generally designed for no-code setup by marketers, though initial site integration may still involve a developer depending on the platform.

use_cases.title: E-commerce conversion tracking
use_cases.description: Store owners use pixel managers to track add-to-cart and purchase events for ad platform optimization.

use_cases.title: Agency pixel management across client sites
use_cases.description: Agencies use centralized pixel tools to manage tracking for multiple client websites from one place.

enrichment.short_description: ClickUp is an all-in-one work management platform combining tasks, docs, chat, whiteboards, goals, and AI features for teams that want to replace multiple point tools with one app.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ClickUp is an all-in-one project management platform with tasks, docs, chat, and AI. See pricing plans, key features, pros and cons, and who it's best for.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ClickUp is a cloud-based work and project management platform founded in 2017 by Zeb Evans (CEO) and Alex Yurkowski (CTO). The company relocated its headquarters to San Diego, California, in January 2020 and has since raised significant venture funding, reaching unicorn status in 2021 at a roughly $4 billion valuation with backers including a $400 million Series C round. ClickUp positions itself as an all-in-one replacement for separate project management, docs, chat, spreadsheet, and time-tracking tools, unifying tasks, Docs, Whiteboards, Chat, Goals, Dashboards, and an AI assistant called ClickUp Brain inside a single workspace. The platform is built around customizable 'Spaces,' 'Folders,' and 'Lists' that hold tasks, and it supports multiple views of the same work — List, Board (Kanban), Calendar, Gantt, Timeline, Mind Map, and Table views — so different teams (engineering, marketing, operations, agencies) can visualize the same underlying data differently. ClickUp offers a Free Forever plan with unlimited tasks and members but capped storage (60MB) and limited automations. Paid tiers include Unlimited at $7/user/month (billed annually) which removes storage caps and adds Gantt charts, native time tracking, goals, and integrations; Business at $12/user/month (billed annually), the most popular paid tier, which adds unlimited dashboards, timeline views, webhooks, mind maps, and higher automation limits; and Enterprise, which uses custom pricing and adds SAML SSO, enterprise API access, white-labeling, HIPAA/MSA compliance options, and a dedicated customer success manager. ClickUp Brain, the platform's AI layer, is sold as an add-on starting around $9/user/month (annual billing) for core AI writing and assistant features, with a higher 'Everything AI' tier around $28/user/month (annual) adding AI notetaking, image generation, and expanded automation credits. ClickUp is available as a web app and has native desktop apps for Mac and Windows plus mobile apps for iOS and Android, and it integrates with tools such as Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, Figma, and Zoom. It is aimed at a broad range of users — from solo freelancers and small startups on the free plan up to mid-market and enterprise teams needing custom permissions, security controls, and portfolio-level reporting across many projects. Because ClickUp packs an unusually large number of features and configuration options into one product, teams that want a simple, minimal tool sometimes report a steeper learning curve than single-purpose apps like a plain to-do list or a dedicated time tracker; the tradeoff is fewer separate subscriptions and a single source of truth for work data across a company.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ClickUp is a cloud-based productivity and project management platform founded in 2017 by Zeb Evans and Alex Yurkowski, now headquartered in San Diego, California. It markets itself as a single app to replace disparate tools for task management, documentation, chat, and time tracking.

The platform serves teams ranging from solo freelancers to large enterprises, offering configurable workspaces (Spaces, Folders, Lists) and more than a dozen ways to view the same tasks, from Kanban boards to Gantt charts and mind maps.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include task management with custom statuses and fields, multiple task views (List, Board, Gantt, Calendar, Timeline, Table, Mind Map), Docs for collaborative documentation, Whiteboards for visual planning, native time tracking, Goals for OKR-style tracking, and Dashboards for reporting.
ClickUp Brain adds AI-powered writing assistance, task summarization, an in-app knowledge assistant, and automation suggestions across the workspace as a paid add-on.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ClickUp's Free Forever plan supports unlimited tasks and members with 60MB of storage and limited automations. Paid plans are Unlimited ($7/user/month annual), Business ($12/user/month annual), and Enterprise (custom pricing).

ClickUp Brain AI is a separate add-on, starting around $9/user/month (annual billing) for core AI features, with an expanded 'Everything AI' tier around $28/user/month for heavier AI usage including notetaking and image generation.

features.title: Multiple task views
features.description: Switch between List, Board, Gantt, Calendar, Timeline, Table, and Mind Map views of the same underlying tasks.

features.title: Docs and Whiteboards
features.description: Built-in collaborative documents and visual whiteboards for planning without leaving the platform.

features.title: Native time tracking
features.description: Track time directly on tasks without a separate time-tracking app, available from the Unlimited plan up.

features.title: Goals and Dashboards
features.description: Set measurable goals and build custom dashboards to report progress across teams and projects.

features.title: ClickUp Brain (AI)
features.description: An AI add-on for writing assistance, task summaries, and workspace-wide question answering.

features.title: Automations
features.description: Rule-based automations to move tasks, assign owners, and update statuses, with usage limits scaling by plan.

features.title: Custom fields and statuses
features.description: Tailor task metadata and workflow stages to match team-specific processes.

pros: Extremely broad feature set that can replace several separate point tools.
Generous free plan with unlimited tasks and members.
Highly configurable views and permissions suited to many team types.
Frequent product updates and an active AI feature roadmap.
cons: The breadth of features creates a steeper learning curve than simpler single-purpose tools.
AI features (ClickUp Brain) are priced as add-ons rather than bundled into base plans.
Free plan storage is limited (60MB), pushing active teams toward paid tiers quickly.
Some advanced reporting and security controls are reserved for Business and Enterprise tiers.

faqs.question: Does ClickUp have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClickUp offers a Free Forever plan with unlimited tasks and members, though storage is capped at 60MB and automation runs are limited.

faqs.question: How much does ClickUp cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $7/user/month (Unlimited, billed annually), with Business at $12/user/month and custom Enterprise pricing. AI features are a separate add-on starting around $9/user/month.

faqs.question: Is ClickUp good for large enterprises?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan adds SAML SSO, enterprise API access, white-labeling, HIPAA/MSA compliance options, and a dedicated customer success manager for larger organizations.

faqs.question: Does ClickUp have mobile and desktop apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClickUp has native apps for Mac and Windows as well as iOS and Android mobile apps, in addition to its web app.

faqs.question: Who founded ClickUp?
faqs.answer: ClickUp was founded in 2017 by Zeb Evans, who serves as CEO, and Alex Yurkowski, who serves as CTO.

use_cases.title: Cross-functional project management
use_cases.description: Teams use ClickUp to plan, assign, and track work across marketing, product, and operations in one workspace.

use_cases.title: Agency and client work tracking
use_cases.description: Agencies use ClickUp's Spaces and custom permissions to manage multiple client projects with separate visibility.

use_cases.title: Solo and small-team task management
use_cases.description: Freelancers and small startups use the free plan for personal and team to-do tracking without upfront cost.

enrichment.short_description: ClientSuccess is a B2B SaaS customer success platform that combines health scoring, renewal forecasting, onboarding playbooks, and executive reporting to help teams reduce churn.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ClientSuccess is a customer success platform with health scores, renewal forecasting, and CSM playbooks built for B2B SaaS teams. See features, pricing tiers, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ClientSuccess is a customer success management (CSM) platform built specifically for B2B SaaS companies, founded in 2014 by Dave Blake, who previously served as Vice President of Global Customer Success at Adobe. The company is headquartered in Lehi, Utah — part of the 'Silicon Slopes' tech corridor — and operates as a privately held company with roughly 40 employees, having raised approximately $5.8 million in funding. ClientSuccess was built explicitly 'by CSMs, for CSMs,' meaning its workflows are designed around the day-to-day job of a customer success manager rather than being a repurposed CRM or support desk. The platform's core capability is a composite customer health score (branded SuccessScore) that blends signals such as product usage data, NPS/sentiment responses, support ticket volume, billing status, and executive engagement, with each signal's weighting configurable by the customer's own CS team. Beyond health scoring, ClientSuccess provides renewal and expansion forecasting to help predict revenue retention, a unified 360-degree customer view that pulls in data from CRM, support desk, and product analytics integrations, and lifecycle playbooks — templated task sequences and communications for onboarding, quarterly business reviews (QBRs), and save/win-back motions. The platform has also introduced an AI layer branded SmartCS, an AI co-pilot intended to surface insights, recommend next-best actions, and automate parts of a CSM's workflow such as drafting update summaries. ClientSuccess does not publish per-seat or per-customer pricing on its public site; instead it sells three main packages — Startup, Growth, and Enterprise — that scale by number of user licenses and number of customer accounts managed, sold as an annual SaaS subscription following a quote-based sales process. The company positions its pricing as typically 30-50% less expensive than larger enterprise customer success platforms such as Gainsight, while targeting SaaS companies roughly in the $5 million to $500 million ARR range — meaning it is generally not aimed at very early-stage startups with no dedicated CS function, nor at the very largest enterprise CS organizations that Gainsight or ChurnZero might serve. Typical buyers are VPs of Customer Success, Chief Customer Officers, and individual CSMs at subscription software companies who need a system of record for account health, renewal risk, and expansion opportunity that is lighter-weight and faster to implement than a full enterprise CS suite. Because pricing and some feature depth are gated behind a sales conversation, prospective buyers should request a live demo and quote to confirm current package details, integration coverage (common integrations include Salesforce, HubSpot, Zendesk, and Intercom), and implementation timelines before committing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ClientSuccess is a customer success management platform founded in 2014 by Dave Blake and headquartered in Lehi, Utah. It is purpose-built for B2B SaaS companies that need to track customer health, forecast renewals, and manage onboarding and expansion motions.

The platform is designed around the daily workflow of customer success managers (CSMs) rather than being adapted from a general CRM, and it is typically positioned as a lighter-weight, more affordable alternative to larger enterprise CS platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ClientSuccess centers on a configurable customer health score (SuccessScore) that combines usage, sentiment, support, billing, and engagement signals, plus renewal and expansion forecasting to flag at-risk accounts ahead of contract dates.

Other core capabilities include a unified 360-degree customer view via CRM/support/product-analytics integrations, templated lifecycle playbooks for onboarding and QBRs, and an AI co-pilot (SmartCS) for surfacing insights and drafting updates.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ClientSuccess does not publish self-serve pricing. It sells three packages — Startup, Growth, and Enterprise — priced by license count and number of managed customer accounts, sold as an annual subscription following a sales quote.
The company positions its pricing as generally lower than large enterprise CS suites like Gainsight, targeting SaaS companies roughly between $5M and $500M in annual recurring revenue.

features.title: SuccessScore health scoring
features.description: A composite customer health score combining usage, sentiment/NPS, support activity, billing, and engagement signals.

features.title: Renewal & expansion forecasting
features.description: Forecasting tools to flag at-risk renewals and surface upsell/expansion opportunities.

features.title: 360-degree customer view
features.description: Unified account view pulling data from CRM, support desk, and product analytics integrations.

features.title: Lifecycle playbooks
features.description: Templated task sequences and communications for onboarding, QBRs, and save motions.

features.title: SmartCS AI co-pilot
features.description: AI-driven insight surfacing and workflow automation for customer success managers.

features.title: Executive reporting
features.description: Dashboards and reporting built for CS leadership and executive stakeholders.

pros: Purpose-built specifically for B2B SaaS customer success teams rather than a general CRM.
Configurable health scoring that blends multiple data sources into one metric.
Positioned as more affordable than large enterprise CS platforms like Gainsight.
Strong focus on renewal/expansion forecasting for revenue retention.
cons: Pricing is not published and requires a sales conversation to obtain a quote.
Primarily aimed at established SaaS companies, so very early-stage startups may find it more than they need.
Feature depth for the largest enterprise CS organizations may lag more mature platforms like Gainsight.

faqs.question: What is ClientSuccess used for?
faqs.answer: ClientSuccess is a customer success management platform used by B2B SaaS companies to track customer health, forecast renewals, manage onboarding, and reduce churn.

faqs.question: How much does ClientSuccess cost?
faqs.answer: ClientSuccess does not publish public pricing. It sells Startup, Growth, and Enterprise packages priced by license and account count through a sales quote process.

faqs.question: Who founded ClientSuccess?
faqs.answer: ClientSuccess was founded in 2014 by Dave Blake, a former VP of Global Customer Success at Adobe.

faqs.question: Does ClientSuccess integrate with CRMs like Salesforce?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClientSuccess is designed to integrate with CRM, support desk, and product analytics tools to build a unified customer view.

faqs.question: Is ClientSuccess suitable for early-stage startups?
faqs.answer: ClientSuccess is generally positioned for SaaS companies in the roughly $5M to $500M ARR range, so very early-stage startups without a dedicated CS function may find simpler tools more suitable.

use_cases.title: Churn reduction and renewal management
use_cases.description: CS teams use health scores and renewal forecasting to identify at-risk accounts before contracts come up for renewal.

use_cases.title: Structured onboarding
use_cases.description: Teams use lifecycle playbooks to standardize onboarding steps and communications for new customers.

use_cases.title: Executive revenue retention reporting
use_cases.description: CS leadership uses dashboards to report account health and retention trends to executives.
use_cases.audience: VPs of Customer Success / Chief Customer Officers

enrichment.short_description: Cliniko is cloud-based practice management software for allied health clinics, handling appointment scheduling, patient records, clinical notes, telehealth, and billing in one system.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cliniko is practice management software for allied health clinics with scheduling, patient records, telehealth, and billing. See pricing by practitioner count and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Cliniko is cloud-based practice management software for allied health practitioners — including physiotherapists, chiropractors, psychologists, podiatrists, and similar clinics — founded in 2011 (with the company noting pricing has stayed on the same structure since June that year) by co-founders Joel Friedlaender and Liora Dafner-Beach in Melbourne, Australia, where it remains headquartered and privately, wholly owned by its original founders. The company employs roughly 60 people distributed globally. Cliniko's product covers the operational core of running a clinic: appointment scheduling with an online patient booking widget, patient records and configurable clinical note templates, treatment notes, telehealth video consultations, invoicing and payment processing, and integrated SMS/email appointment reminders. Rather than segmenting features behind multiple pricing tiers, Cliniko uses a single-tier, practitioner-count-based pricing model: every subscriber gets the full feature set, unlimited admin/reception users, unlimited patient records, and unlimited file storage, with the monthly price scaling purely by how many practitioners are on the account. As of the current published pricing, a solo practitioner pays $45/month, 2-5 practitioners pay $95/month, 6-8 practitioners pay $145/month, 9-12 practitioners pay $195/month, 13-25 practitioners pay $295/month, and clinics with 26-200 practitioners pay $395/month (in the currency of the subscribing region — Cliniko publishes pricing in AUD, USD, GBP, and EUR depending on the customer's location). Cliniko offers a 30-day free trial that requires no credit card, and annual billing includes one additional free month compared to paying monthly. Optional add-on costs include SMS messaging at roughly 10 cents per message via a pre-paid credit system, and some third-party integrations may carry separate fees from those providers. Cliniko donates roughly 2% of subscription revenue to charity as a stated company practice. The platform is used by solo practitioners up through larger multi-location allied health groups, and it is positioned specifically for allied health rather than for general medical practices, dental clinics, or hospital systems — its feature set (treatment notes, outcome measures, health fund claiming in supported regions) reflects that focus. Because Cliniko's pricing has intentionally remained simple and flat-feature since its early years, prospective buyers do not need to compare feature matrices across tiers the way they would with most SaaS products — the primary purchasing decision is simply how many practitioners will use the account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Cliniko is cloud-based practice management software built for allied health clinics — physiotherapy, chiropractic, psychology, podiatry, and similar practices. It was founded in 2011 by Joel Friedlaender and Liora Dafner-Beach and remains headquartered in Melbourne, Australia as a privately, founder-owned company.
Unlike many SaaS products that gate features behind pricing tiers, Cliniko offers the same full feature set to every subscriber and scales price purely by the number of practitioners on the account.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core functionality includes appointment scheduling with an online patient booking widget, configurable clinical note and treatment note templates, patient record management, telehealth video consultations, invoicing, and automated SMS/email appointment reminders.
All subscription levels include unlimited admin/reception users, unlimited locations, unlimited patient records, and unlimited file storage — features are not split across tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Cliniko prices by practitioner count rather than feature tier: $45/month for 1 practitioner, $95/month for 2-5, $145/month for 6-8, $195/month for 9-12, $295/month for 13-25, and $395/month for 26-200 practitioners.

A 30-day free trial requiring no credit card is available, and annual billing includes one bonus free month compared to monthly billing. SMS reminders are billed separately at roughly 10 cents per message via prepaid credits.

features.title: Appointment scheduling
features.description: Calendar-based scheduling with an online booking widget patients can use to self-book appointments.

features.title: Clinical & treatment notes
features.description: Configurable note templates suited to allied health documentation needs.

features.title: Telehealth
features.description: Built-in video consultation capability for remote appointments.

features.title: Invoicing & payments
features.description: Integrated billing and payment processing tied to appointments and patient accounts.

features.title: Automated reminders
features.description: SMS and email appointment reminders to reduce no-shows, billed as prepaid SMS credits.

features.title: Unlimited storage and users
features.description: Every plan includes unlimited admin users, patient records, and file storage regardless of price tier.

pros: Simple, transparent pricing based only on practitioner count — no feature-gated tiers to compare.
Full feature set available even on the lowest-priced solo plan.
Purpose-built for allied health workflows rather than adapted from general medical software.
30-day free trial with no credit card required.
cons: Focused specifically on allied health, so it may not fit general medical, dental, or hospital-scale practices.
SMS reminders are a metered add-on cost rather than included in the base subscription.
No free-forever plan; a paid subscription is required after the trial ends.

faqs.question: How much does Cliniko cost?
faqs.answer: Cliniko prices by practitioner count: $45/month for a solo practitioner, scaling up to $395/month for clinics with 26-200 practitioners. All tiers include the same full feature set.

faqs.question: Does Cliniko offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cliniko offers a 30-day free trial that does not require a credit card to start.

faqs.question: Who is Cliniko designed for?
faqs.answer: Cliniko is designed for allied health practitioners such as physiotherapists, chiropractors, psychologists, and podiatrists rather than general medical or dental practices.

faqs.question: Does Cliniko support telehealth?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cliniko includes built-in telehealth video consultation functionality.

faqs.question: Who founded Cliniko?
faqs.answer: Cliniko was founded by Joel Friedlaender and Liora Dafner-Beach in Melbourne, Australia, and remains wholly owned by its original founders.

use_cases.title: Solo allied health practice management
use_cases.description: Independent practitioners use Cliniko to manage scheduling, notes, and billing in one system at the entry-level price tier.

use_cases.title: Multi-practitioner clinic operations
use_cases.description: Growing clinics use Cliniko's practitioner-based pricing to add staff without losing access to any features.

use_cases.title: Remote/telehealth consultations
use_cases.description: Practices use Cliniko's built-in telehealth to see patients remotely alongside in-person appointments.

enrichment.short_description: Clio is cloud-based legal practice management software for law firms, covering time tracking, billing, trust accounting, client intake, and document management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clio is cloud-based legal practice management software with billing, trust accounting, and client intake. Compare EasyStart, Essentials, Advanced, and Complete plans.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Clio, operated by Themis Solutions Inc., is cloud-based legal practice management software founded in 2007 by Jack Newton and Rian Gauvreau. The company is headquartered in Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada, and has grown into a large legal technology employer with more than 2,000 employees across offices including Toronto, Calgary, London, Manchester, Dublin, Sydney, Barcelona, and Bogotá; Clio has been recognized as one of Canada's Top 100 Employers. The company reports serving more than 400,000 legal professionals across over 130 countries, making it one of the most widely adopted practice management platforms in the legal industry. Clio's core product, Clio Manage, centralizes the day-to-day operations of running a law firm: client and matter management, calendaring and court-date tracking, time and expense tracking, document management and templates, e-signatures, and billing, invoicing, and trust (IOLTA) accounting that follows jurisdiction-specific compliance rules. The company also sells companion products, including Clio Grow for client intake and lead management, and has invested in AI features (marketed under the Clio Duo/AI drafting umbrella) for drafting and summarizing legal documents in its higher tiers. Clio Manage is sold in four tiers billed per user per month on an annual basis: EasyStart at $49/user/month covering basic time tracking, billing, and trust accounting for solo practitioners; Essentials at $89/user/month, described as the firm's most popular tier, adding fuller practice management workflows; Advanced at $119/user/month, aimed at established small-to-mid-size firms and adding unlimited e-signatures, advanced reporting, and priority support; and Complete at $149/user/month, the top tier, bundling client intake automation and AI drafting capabilities alongside full practice management. Monthly (non-annual) billing typically costs $10-20 more per user per month than the listed annual rates. Firms often incur additional costs beyond the base subscription, such as Clio Payments processing fees (around 2.95% plus $0.20 per transaction) and optional integrations like QuickBooks Online. Clio is available as a web application with iOS and Android mobile apps, and it integrates with widely used legal and accounting tools. It targets law firms of essentially any size, from solo attorneys on EasyStart up to larger multi-partner firms on Advanced or Complete that need e-signatures, advanced reporting, and AI-assisted drafting. Because Clio bundles trust accounting compliance features directly into its billing module, it is particularly relevant to firms in jurisdictions with strict client-fund handling rules, which is a key differentiator versus general-purpose practice or project management software not built for legal-specific compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Clio, built by Themis Solutions Inc., is cloud-based legal practice management software founded in 2007 by Jack Newton and Rian Gauvreau and headquartered in Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada. The company reports serving more than 400,000 legal professionals in over 130 countries.

Clio Manage, the flagship product, centralizes client and matter management, time tracking, billing, trust accounting, and document management for law firms, while companion product Clio Grow covers client intake and lead management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core capabilities include matter and client management, calendaring with court-date tracking, time and expense tracking, document management with templates and e-signatures, and billing/invoicing with jurisdiction-aware trust (IOLTA) accounting.
Higher tiers add unlimited e-signatures, advanced reporting, client intake automation via Clio Grow, and AI-assisted document drafting capabilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Clio Manage is priced in four annually billed tiers: EasyStart at $49/user/month, Essentials at $89/user/month (most popular), Advanced at $119/user/month, and Complete at $149/user/month. Monthly billing costs roughly $10-20 more per user than the annual rate.
Additional costs can include Clio Payments processing fees (about 2.95% + $0.20 per transaction) and third-party integrations such as QuickBooks Online.

features.title: Matter & client management
features.description: Centralized records for clients, matters, and related documents and communications.

features.title: Time & billing
features.description: Time and expense tracking tied directly to invoicing and billing workflows.

features.title: Trust accounting
features.description: Jurisdiction-aware IOLTA/trust accounting built into the billing module for compliant client-fund handling.

features.title: Document management & e-signatures
features.description: Document templates, storage, and e-signature collection, with unlimited e-signatures on higher tiers.

features.title: Client intake (Clio Grow)
features.description: A companion product for lead management and automated client intake, bundled into the Complete tier.

features.title: AI drafting
features.description: AI-assisted drafting and summarization features available on the Complete plan.

features.title: Calendaring & court-date tracking
features.description: Legal-specific calendaring that helps firms track filing and court deadlines.

pros: Purpose-built trust accounting compliance features not found in general practice management tools.
Wide adoption (400,000+ legal professionals) and a large integration ecosystem.
Tiered pricing lets solo attorneys and larger firms both find an appropriate plan.
Companion products (Clio Grow) extend the platform into client intake without switching tools.
cons: Pricing rises significantly between tiers, with AI drafting and full intake automation reserved for the top Complete tier.
Monthly (non-annual) billing carries a notable price premium over annual billing.
Additional costs such as payment processing fees and third-party integrations add to total cost of ownership.
Feature scope may be more than very small or informal legal practices need.

faqs.question: How much does Clio cost?
faqs.answer: Clio Manage starts at $49/user/month for EasyStart (annual billing), with Essentials at $89, Advanced at $119, and Complete at $149/user/month.

faqs.question: Who founded Clio?
faqs.answer: Clio was founded in 2007 by Jack Newton and Rian Gauvreau and is operated by Themis Solutions Inc., headquartered in Burnaby, British Columbia, Canada.

faqs.question: Does Clio handle trust accounting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clio includes jurisdiction-aware trust (IOLTA) accounting built into its billing module, which is a key feature for law firms handling client funds.

faqs.question: What is Clio Grow?
faqs.answer: Clio Grow is Clio's companion product for client intake and lead management, bundled into the Complete plan and available as an add-on to other plans.

faqs.question: Does Clio have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clio's Complete plan includes AI-assisted drafting and document summarization capabilities.

faqs.question: Is Clio available on mobile?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clio has iOS and Android mobile apps in addition to its web application.

use_cases.title: Solo attorney practice management
use_cases.description: Solo practitioners use Clio's EasyStart plan for basic time tracking, billing, and trust accounting.

use_cases.title: Growing multi-attorney firms
use_cases.description: Established small-to-mid-size firms use Advanced or Complete for e-signatures, advanced reporting, and AI drafting.

use_cases.title: Client intake automation
use_cases.description: Firms use Clio Grow to capture and automate lead intake before matters are opened in Clio Manage.
use_cases.audience: Law firms managing high inbound lead volume

enrichment.short_description: ClipBucket is a free, open-source PHP video-sharing platform that lets anyone self-host a YouTube-style site with uploads, playlists, groups, and social features.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ClipBucket is a free, open-source PHP video-sharing script for self-hosting a YouTube-style platform with uploads, playlists, groups, and social features.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ClipBucket is a free, open-source video-sharing platform written in PHP, originally released so that anyone could self-host their own YouTube-style video (and photo) sharing website. The original repository, maintained under the arslancb/clipbucket project, grew into what its documentation describes as one of the fastest-growing video-sharing scripts of its era, adding social features on top of core video hosting: user groups, playlists, collections, friend requests, and private messaging, so that operators could run either a dedicated video site, a photo-sharing site, or both from the same install. Because it is self-hosted and open-source, ClipBucket carries no licensing fee — operators only pay for their own server hosting, storage, and bandwidth, which scale with content volume and audience size. The original ClipBucket project slowed after its 4.1 release (May 2018) and was archived in December 2022, but the codebase has been carried forward by community forks, most notably ClipBucket V5, which has continued shipping consolidation and stability releases (for example, version 5.5.2) focused on security hardening and general robustness rather than major new feature pushes. Running a ClipBucket instance requires a standard LAMP-style hosting environment: MySQL 5.6+ or MariaDB 10.3+ for the database, PHP 8.0+ with specific extensions (mysqli, curl, xml, mbstring, gd, openssl, fileinfo) and the ability to run shell/exec functions, FFmpeg 3+ for video transcoding, and mediainfo plus a working sendmail setup for notifications. Deployment is typically manual — uploading files, configuring the database, and setting file permissions on a VPS or dedicated server — though community-maintained Docker images exist to simplify setup for operators comfortable with containers. Because it depends on shell/exec access and FFmpeg for transcoding, ClipBucket is not well suited to typical shared hosting plans that restrict those capabilities; it is aimed at technically capable operators — developers, small media companies, niche community builders, or hobbyists — who want full control over their video platform's code, data, and monetization rather than using a hosted SaaS video platform. As an older, community-maintained open-source project, prospective adopters should evaluate the currency of whichever fork they choose (the original arslancb repository is archived, while ClipBucket V5 remains under active community development) and should expect to handle their own server security, backups, and scaling rather than relying on a vendor-provided SLA, which is the fundamental tradeoff of a self-hosted open-source video platform versus a hosted commercial alternative.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ClipBucket is a free, open-source PHP script for self-hosting a video-sharing website, often described as a YouTube-clone script. It lets operators run their own branded video (and optionally photo) sharing platform on their own server.

The original project (arslancb/clipbucket) was archived in December 2022 after slowing following its 4.1 release, but the codebase continues under community forks, most notably ClipBucket V5, which ships ongoing stability and security releases.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ClipBucket includes core video hosting (upload, transcode via FFmpeg, playback) alongside social features such as playlists, collections, user groups, friend requests, and private messaging, enabling both video-sharing and lightweight social-network functionality.
Because it is self-hosted, operators have full control of data, moderation, and monetization, but must also manage their own server infrastructure, security patching, and scaling.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: ClipBucket itself is free and open-source with no licensing fee. The only costs are for self-managed server hosting, storage, and bandwidth, which scale with the size of the video library and audience.

features.title: Video upload & transcoding
features.description: Handles video uploads and transcodes them via FFmpeg for web playback.

features.title: Playlists & collections
features.description: Users can organize uploaded videos into playlists and collections.

features.title: Social features
features.description: Includes user groups, friend requests, and private messaging alongside video hosting.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs on a standard LAMP-style stack (PHP, MySQL/MariaDB, FFmpeg) under the operator's full control.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: Source is freely available to modify, extend, or audit, with community forks such as ClipBucket V5.

pros: Completely free and open-source with no licensing cost.
Full control over hosting, data, moderation, and monetization.
Includes social features (groups, messaging, playlists) beyond basic video hosting.
Community forks (ClipBucket V5) continue active development after the original project was archived.
cons: Requires technical setup: PHP, MySQL/MariaDB, FFmpeg, and shell/exec access, which rules out most shared hosting.
The original arslancb repository is archived, so evaluating which fork is actively maintained takes extra diligence.
No vendor support or SLA — operators are responsible for their own security, backups, and scaling.

faqs.question: Is ClipBucket free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ClipBucket is free and open-source. The only costs are for self-managed server hosting, storage, and bandwidth.

faqs.question: What server requirements does ClipBucket have?
faqs.answer: It requires PHP 8.0+ with specific extensions, MySQL 5.6+ or MariaDB 10.3+, FFmpeg 3+ for transcoding, and mediainfo, plus a working sendmail configuration.

faqs.question: Is the original ClipBucket project still maintained?
faqs.answer: The original arslancb/clipbucket repository was archived in December 2022, but community forks such as ClipBucket V5 continue to ship stability and security releases.

faqs.question: Can ClipBucket run on shared hosting?
faqs.answer: It's generally not well suited to typical shared hosting because it needs shell/exec access and FFmpeg for video transcoding, which most shared hosts restrict.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted community video site
use_cases.description: Niche communities and hobbyist operators use ClipBucket to run their own branded video-sharing site outside major platforms.

use_cases.title: Custom video platform for developers
use_cases.description: Developers use ClipBucket's open-source codebase as a starting point to build a customized video platform.

enrichment.short_description: Clipdrop is an AI-powered photo and image editing toolkit — background removal, relighting, cleanup, upscaling, and image generation — originally built by Init ML and now owned by Jasper.
enrichment.seo_title: Clipdrop Review: AI Image Editing Features & Pricing (2026)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clipdrop is an AI image editing suite with background removal, relighting, and upscaling. See features, pricing, and who owns Clipdrop today.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Clipdrop is an AI-powered photo and image editing toolkit founded in 2020 by Google alumni Cyril Diagne, Damien Henry, and Jonathan Blanchet under the company name Init ML, headquartered in Paris, France. Clipdrop grew quickly, reportedly reaching more than 15 million users, offering tools such as Remove Background, Cleanup (object removal), Relight, Uncrop (generative expansion of an image's aspect ratio), Reimagine (generating variations of a photo), an image Upscaler, Replace Background, and Stable Doodle (sketch-to-image), along with direct access to Stability AI's Stable Diffusion XL for text-to-image generation. Ownership of Clipdrop has changed twice in recent years. On March 7, 2023, Stability AI acquired Init ML/Clipdrop for an undisclosed price, with Clipdrop operating as a wholly owned subsidiary and its founders taking on VP-level roles at Stability AI; this integration gave Clipdrop's tools deeper ties to Stability AI's generative models. Then, on February 20, 2024, Jasper (the AI marketing content platform) acquired Clipdrop from Stability AI, again for an undisclosed price, with the stated goal of strengthening Jasper's multimodal, image-generation capabilities for enterprise marketing teams; the Clipdrop team continued operating from its Paris base as part of Jasper's expansion into Europe. Individual users can still access and purchase Clipdrop as a standalone product through clipdrop.co, while business customers can access its capabilities through the Jasper API, with Jasper planning deeper integration of Clipdrop's tools into its broader AI copilot over time. On pricing, Clipdrop offers a free tier that includes a limited number of processed images per month (commonly cited as around 15 images) with a watermark, and a Pro subscription at $9/month that removes watermarks, raises resolution to HD, and lifts usage limits. For developers, Clipdrop also offers metered API pricing — figures commonly cited include roughly $0.002 per background-removal call and $0.005 per Stable Diffusion XL generation — with tiered API key rate limits (Free around 10 requests/minute, Pro around 60 requests/minute, and custom Enterprise limits up to roughly 1,000 requests/second). Clipdrop's standout feature relative to many competing background-removal tools is Relight, which lets a user add, move, and adjust virtual light sources on an uploaded portrait or product photo after the fact. The product is accessible via web browser, mobile apps, and a Photoshop plugin, making it useful for e-commerce sellers needing clean product photos, marketers needing quick image edits without a full design tool, and developers who want to embed background removal or image generation into their own applications via the API.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Clipdrop is an AI-powered photo and image editing toolkit founded in 2020 in Paris, France by Cyril Diagne, Damien Henry, and Jonathan Blanchet under the company Init ML. Stability AI acquired Clipdrop in March 2023, and Jasper subsequently acquired it from Stability AI in February 2024.

Individual users can still buy Clipdrop as a standalone product at clipdrop.co, while business customers can access its capabilities through the Jasper API as part of Jasper's broader AI marketing platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Clipdrop's toolkit includes Remove Background, Cleanup (object removal), Relight (adjustable virtual lighting on portraits/products), Uncrop (generative aspect-ratio expansion), Reimagine (photo variation generation), an image Upscaler, Replace Background, and Stable Doodle (sketch-to-image), plus direct Stable Diffusion XL text-to-image generation.
Relight is frequently cited as Clipdrop's standout differentiator, allowing users to add, move, and adjust light sources on an existing photo after it was taken.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Clipdrop offers a free tier with a limited number of processed images per month and a watermark, and a Pro plan at $9/month that removes the watermark, unlocks HD output, and lifts usage limits.

Developers can access Clipdrop via a metered API, with per-call pricing (roughly $0.002 for background removal, $0.005 for SDXL generation) and tiered rate limits across Free, Pro, and Enterprise API keys.

features.title: Background removal
features.description: Automatically removes photo backgrounds with clean edges, including bulk processing of multiple images at once.

features.title: Relight
features.description: Lets users add, move, and adjust virtual light sources on a portrait or product photo after it was taken.

features.title: Cleanup
features.description: Removes unwanted objects, text, or blemishes from a photo.

features.title: Uncrop
features.description: Generatively expands an image's canvas or aspect ratio beyond its original borders.

features.title: Image Upscaler
features.description: Increases image resolution using AI upscaling.

features.title: Stable Diffusion XL generation
features.description: Direct access to text-to-image generation via Stability AI's SDXL model.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Metered API access to background removal, upscaling, and image generation for embedding in other applications.

pros: Broad, focused toolkit for common image-editing tasks without needing a full design suite.
Relight is a distinctive feature not commonly offered by competing background-removal tools.
Accessible via web, mobile apps, and a Photoshop plugin, plus a developer API.
Free tier available for casual or low-volume use.
cons: Ownership has changed twice since 2023 (Stability AI, then Jasper), which may create uncertainty about long-term standalone product direction.
Free tier is limited in volume and adds a watermark to output.
Advanced/business use requires either the Pro subscription or a separate metered API relationship via Jasper.

faqs.question: Who owns Clipdrop now?
faqs.answer: Clipdrop is currently owned by Jasper, which acquired it from Stability AI on February 20, 2024. Stability AI had itself acquired Clipdrop (via its maker, Init ML) in March 2023.

faqs.question: Is Clipdrop free?
faqs.answer: Clipdrop has a free tier with a limited number of processed images per month and a watermark; a Pro plan at $9/month removes the watermark and unlocks HD, unlimited-style usage.

faqs.question: What is Clipdrop's Relight feature?
faqs.answer: Relight lets users add, move, and adjust virtual light sources on an already-taken portrait or product photo, a feature not commonly found in competing tools.

faqs.question: Can developers use Clipdrop via an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, Clipdrop offers a metered developer API with per-call pricing for features like background removal and Stable Diffusion XL image generation, with tiered rate limits.

faqs.question: Who founded Clipdrop?
faqs.answer: Clipdrop was founded in 2020 by Google alumni Cyril Diagne, Damien Henry, and Jonathan Blanchet under the company Init ML, based in Paris, France.

use_cases.title: E-commerce product photo editing
use_cases.description: Sellers use Clipdrop's background removal and relighting to produce clean, consistent product photos.

use_cases.title: Quick marketing image edits
use_cases.description: Marketers use Clipdrop for fast object removal, upscaling, and background changes without a full design tool.

use_cases.title: Embedded image processing
use_cases.description: Developers use the Clipdrop API to add background removal or AI image generation into their own applications.

enrichment.short_description: Clockify is a time-tracking and timesheet platform from CAKE.com with reporting, invoicing, and 100+ integrations, offering a free plan for small teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clockify is a time-tracking app with timesheets, reporting, and invoicing. Compare Free, Basic, Standard, Pro, and Enterprise plans, features, and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Clockify is a time-tracking and timesheet platform launched in 2017 by the company now branded CAKE.com Inc. (previously known as COING), headquartered at 2100 Geng Road in Palo Alto, California, with a privately held team of over 200 employees. Clockify was built around a simple pitch — offer time tracking to unlimited users for free — and it grew into the most widely used product in what is now the CAKE.com productivity suite, which also includes Pumble (team chat) and Plaky (project management). As of the current pricing structure, Clockify's Free plan is capped at 5 users (a change introduced in April 2026; the plan previously supported unlimited users on the free tier) and includes unlimited time tracking, timers, manual time entry, timesheets, an auto-tracker, calendar view, mobile/desktop apps, basic reporting, and team activity views, but excludes invoicing, billable rates, project budgets, timesheet approvals, GPS tracking, and audit logs. Paid tiers scale up from there: Basic at $3.99/user/month (annual) or $4.99 (monthly) adds a kiosk mode, the ability to add time for others, hidden time/pages, required fields, bulk editing, break tracking, time estimates, and import tools; Standard at $5.49/user/month (annual) or $6.99 (monthly) adds time-off tracking, invoicing and recurring invoices, timesheet approval workflows, timesheet locking, attendance/overtime tracking, a manager role, task-level billing rates, and QuickBooks integration; Pro at $7.99/user/month (annual) or $9.99 (monthly) adds scheduling, forecasting, expense tracking, labor cost and profit analysis, budgets and estimates, custom fields, multi-currency support, GPS tracking, screenshots, and email reports; and Enterprise at $11.99/user/month (annual) or $14.99 (monthly) adds single sign-on (SSO), a custom subdomain, SCIM provisioning, control accounts, and an audit log. CAKE.com also sells a bundled plan combining Enterprise-tier Clockify with Pumble and Plaky at $12.99/user/month (annual) or $15.99 (monthly), marketed as saving roughly 53% versus subscribing to the three products separately. Clockify supports 90+ third-party integrations spanning project management and communication tools, letting teams embed time tracking into existing workflows rather than switching context. The product is available as a web app plus native desktop apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux, and mobile apps for iOS and Android. Clockify is aimed at freelancers and very small teams on the free tier, growing agencies and consultancies needing invoicing and approvals on Standard, and larger organizations needing budgeting, forecasting, and enterprise security controls (SSO, SCIM, audit logs) on Pro and Enterprise.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Clockify is a time-tracking and timesheet platform launched in 2017 by CAKE.com Inc. (formerly COING), headquartered in Palo Alto, California. It is the flagship product in the CAKE.com productivity suite, alongside team chat app Pumble and project management app Plaky.

Clockify built its reputation on offering time tracking to a large number of users for free; as of April 2026 the free tier is capped at 5 users, with unlimited-user access now reserved for paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core capabilities include timers and manual time entry, timesheets, an auto-tracker for detecting app/website usage, calendar views, and reporting exportable as PDF, CSV, or Excel. Paid tiers layer on invoicing, timesheet approvals, budgeting/forecasting, GPS tracking, and screenshots.
Clockify integrates with 90+ third-party apps for project management and communication, and supports multi-currency billing and QuickBooks integration on Standard tier and above.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Clockify's Free plan (capped at 5 users as of April 2026) includes core tracking and basic reporting. Paid plans are Basic ($3.99/user/month annual), Standard ($5.49), Pro ($7.99), and Enterprise ($11.99), each unlocking progressively more advanced billing, approval, and security features.
A CAKE.com bundle combines Enterprise-tier Clockify with Pumble and Plaky at $12.99/user/month (annual), pitched as a roughly 53% savings versus buying the three products separately.

features.title: Timers & manual entry
features.description: Start/stop timers or log time manually, with an auto-tracker that detects time spent in apps and websites.

features.title: Timesheets & approvals
features.description: Weekly timesheets with locking and manager approval workflows on Standard tier and above.

features.title: Reporting
features.description: Detailed reports by project, client, or team member, exportable as PDF, CSV, or Excel, or shareable via live link.

features.title: Invoicing
features.description: Generate invoices directly from tracked time and expenses, with recurring invoice support.

features.title: Budgeting & forecasting
features.description: Project budgets, cost estimates, and labor cost/profit analysis available on the Pro tier.

features.title: GPS tracking & screenshots
features.description: Location tracking and periodic screenshots for field or remote workforce monitoring on Pro tier.

features.title: 90+ integrations
features.description: Connects with popular project management and communication tools to embed time tracking into existing workflows.

pros: Free plan available for small teams (up to 5 users) with core tracking functionality.
Wide integration library (90+ apps) for embedding time tracking into existing tools.
Granular paid tiers let teams pay only for the billing, approval, or security features they need.
Bundled pricing with Pumble and Plaky offers savings for teams wanting a full productivity suite.
cons: Free plan was reduced from unlimited users to a 5-user cap as of April 2026.
Invoicing, GPS tracking, and advanced reporting are locked behind mid-to-upper paid tiers.
Enterprise security features (SSO, SCIM, audit logs) require the top-priced plan.

faqs.question: Is Clockify free?
faqs.answer: Clockify has a free plan, but as of April 2026 it is capped at 5 users. Larger teams need a paid plan starting at $3.99/user/month (annual billing).

faqs.question: Does Clockify support invoicing?
faqs.answer: Yes, invoicing and recurring invoices are available starting on the Standard plan ($5.49/user/month annual).

faqs.question: Who owns Clockify?
faqs.answer: Clockify is made by CAKE.com Inc. (formerly COING), headquartered in Palo Alto, California, which also owns Pumble and Plaky.

faqs.question: Does Clockify have GPS tracking?
faqs.answer: Yes, GPS tracking is available on the Pro plan ($7.99/user/month annual) along with screenshots and expense tracking.

faqs.question: How many integrations does Clockify support?
faqs.answer: Clockify supports more than 90 third-party integrations across project management and communication tools.

use_cases.title: Freelance and small-team time tracking
use_cases.description: Freelancers and small teams (up to 5 users) use the free plan for basic timers, timesheets, and reporting.

use_cases.title: Agency billing and invoicing
use_cases.description: Agencies use Standard-tier invoicing and approvals to bill clients directly from tracked time.

use_cases.title: Enterprise workforce time management
use_cases.description: Larger organizations use Pro/Enterprise tiers for budgeting, forecasting, GPS tracking, and SSO/audit compliance.
